content.description:
Symbol indexing automatically extracts function, variable, class, and method definitions from code items and stores them as structured metadata in `content._symbols`. This enables symbol-level navigation, transclusion, and code introspection throughout the system.

## Overview

Every code item in Hobson has its symbols parsed and indexed. When you save a code item, the [symbol-indexer-watch](item://ac0ac0ac-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) fires, parses the code with Acorn via [symbol-extractor-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0003-0000-0000-000000000000), and writes the results to `content._symbols` on the item.

This happens silently (no re-render, no re-triggering watchers) and is skipped if symbols haven't changed.

## The `_symbols` Map

The `_symbols` field is a flat map keyed by qualified name:

```javascript
{
  "myFunction": { name: "myFunction", line: 5, col: 0, endLine: 12, endCol: 1, kind: "function", scope: null, signature: "(x, y)" },
  "myFunction.helper": { name: "helper", line: 7, col: 8, endLine: 10, endCol: 3, kind: "variable", scope: "myFunction" },
  "MyClass": { name: "MyClass", line: 14, col: 0, endLine: 30, endCol: 1, kind: "class", scope: null },
  "MyClass.render": { name: "render", line: 15, col: 2, endLine: 20, endCol: 3, kind: "method", scope: "MyClass", signature: "(el)" }
}
```

### Key Format

- Top-level symbols: just the name (`myFunction`)
- Nested symbols: `scope.name` (`MyClass.render`, `createAPI.get`)
- Deeper nesting chains: `a.b.c` for symbols inside `b` inside `a`

### Symbol Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | String | Unqualified name |
| `line` | Number | Start line (1-based) |
| `col` | Number | Start column (0-based) |
| `endLine` | Number | End line (1-based) |
| `endCol` | Number | End column (0-based) |
| `kind` | String | Symbol kind (see below) |
| `scope` | String\|null | Parent scope name, or null for top-level |
| `signature` | String\|undefined | Function signature (only present for function-like symbols) |
| `description` | String\|undefined | JSDoc description (first line of prose before any @tags) |
| `params` | Array\|undefined | JSDoc @param entries: `[{ name, type?, description? }]` |
| `returns` | Object\|undefined | JSDoc @returns entry: `{ type?, description? }` |

### Symbol Kinds

| Kind | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `function` | Function declaration, arrow function, or function expression assigned to a variable |
| `variable` | Variable or const (non-function) |
| `class` | Class declaration |
| `method` | Class method (MethodDefinition) |
| `field` | Class field (non-function PropertyDefinition) |
| `field-function` | Class field assigned a function |
| `property` | Object literal property (non-function) |
| `property-function` | Object literal property assigned a function, or shorthand method |

## How It Works

```
Code item saved
  --> symbol-indexer-watch (item:created / item:updated)
    --> symbol-extractor-lib.extractSymbols(code)
      --> Acorn parses to AST
      --> walkNode() traverses, collecting symbols
      --> attachJSDoc() matches /** */ comments to symbols
    --> Diff against existing _symbols
    --> If changed: silent save to item.content._symbols
```

The watcher only fires for items whose type chain includes `code` (type `22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`).

## Consuming Symbols

### Navigation (scroll to symbol)

When navigating to an item with a `symbol` parameter, the system scrolls to and highlights the symbol's line range:

```javascript
// Programmatic navigation
await api.navigate(itemId, { symbol: 'myFunction' });
await api.navigate(itemId, { field: 'code', symbol: 'MyClass.render' });

// Sibling navigation
api.siblingContainer.addSibling(itemId, { symbol: 'myFunction' });
```

The flow: `api.navigate()` passes `navigateTo` through the rendering context to [generic-view](item://b429b19d-ef0d-4f4f-b2a2-b9e6f80451f2), which forwards `scrollToSymbol` to the code field view ([field-view-code-editable](item://e7b73a8e-2191-4ce5-ae9c-f721b5e30731) or [field-view-code-readonly](item://8e2f3e95-cc2d-44a3-beeb-0569f400da7c)). The field view looks up the symbol in `_symbols` and scrolls to its line range.

When `symbol` is specified without a `field`, the system defaults to the `code` field.

### Transclusion (embed symbol's code)

Embed a specific function or class from another item's code:

```markdown
![](item://some-item-id?symbol=myFunction)
![](item://some-item-id#code?symbol=MyClass.render)
```

[hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) resolves the symbol to a line range and extracts just those lines. When no `#field` is specified, it defaults to `code`.

### Links (navigate to symbol)

Markdown links with symbol parameters open the item scrolled to the symbol:

```markdown
[see myFunction](item://some-item-id?symbol=myFunction)
[MyClass.render](item://some-item-id#code?symbol=MyClass.render)
```

### Code comment links

Inside code editors, comment links also support symbols:

```javascript
// See [render method](item://some-view-id#code?symbol=render)
```

### Introspection (query blocks)

Query blocks can read `_symbols` to generate dynamic documentation:

````markdown
```query
const item = await api.get('some-item-id');
const symbols = item.content._symbols || {};
const fns = Object.entries(symbols)
  .filter(([k, v]) => v.kind === 'function' && !v.scope)
  .map(([k, v]) => `- [\`${v.name}\`](item://${item.id}?symbol=${k}) ${v.signature || '()'}`);
return fns.join('\n');
```
````

The [REPL doc](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) uses this pattern to auto-generate the API method table from kernel:core's `_symbols`.

## Symbol Lookup Resolution

When looking up a symbol by name, consumers try two strategies:

1. **Exact match** on the qualified key (e.g., `MyClass.render`)
2. **Unqualified match** — first symbol whose `name` field matches (e.g., `render` finds `MyClass.render`)

This means you can usually use the short name unless there are ambiguous matches.

## Note on URL Parameters

The `?symbol=` parameter works through `item://` link and transclusion navigation, not as a top-level URL query parameter on `bootloader.html`. For top-level URL navigation, use `?field=code&lines=N-M` instead. See [URL Parameters](item://c0c0c0c0-0031-0000-0000-000000000000) for the bootloader parameters.

## Architecture

| Component | Item | Role |
|-----------|------|------|
| [symbol-extractor-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) | Library | Parses code with Acorn, returns symbol map |
| [symbol-indexer-watch](item://ac0ac0ac-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) | Library (watcher) | Listens for code item changes, runs extractor, saves results |
| [generic-view](item://b429b19d-ef0d-4f4f-b2a2-b9e6f80451f2) | View | Passes `scrollToSymbol` to field views |
| [field-view-code-editable](item://e7b73a8e-2191-4ce5-ae9c-f721b5e30731) | Field view | Scrolls to symbol in editable CodeMirror |
| [field-view-code-readonly](item://8e2f3e95-cc2d-44a3-beeb-0569f400da7c) | Field view | Scrolls to symbol in readonly CodeMirror |
| [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) | Library | Resolves symbols for transclusion and link navigation |

## See Also

- [Content Field Conventions](item://c0c0c0c0-0024-0000-0000-000000000000) — Where `_symbols` fits among standard fields
- [Transclusion](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) — `?symbol=` syntax for partial transclusion
- [Code Execution & Sandboxing](item://c0c0c0c0-0028-0000-0000-000000000000) — How code items are evaluated
- [Event System](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) — How the indexer watcher triggers
